economic_notes: Subsidiaries include Wiikwemkoong Board of Education, Wikwemikong Development Commission, Wikwemikong Tribal Police Service, Wikwemikong Nursing Home, Wikwemikong Property Management Company Limited (Rainbow Ridge Golf Course), and FirstTEL Communications Corporation. Modified equity method entities include Your Dollar Store With More, Great Lakes Hospitality Group Limited Partnership, N'Swakamok Forestry Corporation, 2228293 Ontario Limited, and U.C.C.M Building Supply Limited Partnership. Hotel operations in Little Current, ON. Solar energy investments via Wikwemikong Renewable Energy Limited Partnership. Interest in Ontario First Nations Sovereign Wealth Limited Partnership (Hydro One shares).
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (year ended March 31, 2021). Auditor is Freelandt Caldwell Reilly LLP, Sudbury. Six fully consolidated subsidiaries and five entities accounted for by modified equity method. Net debt position of $10,680,502. Large increase in deferred contributions ($17.5M vs $7.4M prior year) and ISC funding due to COVID-related funding. Tangible capital asset net book value not extractable from the truncated financial statement text provided; balance sheet not fully included in the text. Accounts receivable and current liabilities not determinable from provided text. OSR calculated as total revenue minus total ISC funding only as other government transfers not separately identifiable from 'other revenues'. Robinson Huron Treaty litigation fund guarantee increased post year-end to $1,648,500. GLHGLP in breach of debt covenants at December 31, 2020. expenses_housing estimated as Social Housing Rentals ($4,312,456) plus Housing and Other Projects ($1,330,981). expenses_enterprises estimated as Wikwemikong Development Commission operations ($3,038,447) plus First Nation Enterprise Initiatives ($2,979,255) plus Ontario First Nations LP Distributions ($40,830).
